meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide for Freelancers: Scale Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Master API integration for high-paying clients using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led guide.
content.answer_block: Stop building static interfaces. By integrating robust APIs, you transform basic prototypes into high-value, functional SaaS products. This guide leverages Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, enabling you to ship complex, data-driven applications in hours rather than weeks, significantly increasing your freelance project margins.
content.intro:
The screen glows, but your app feels hollow. You've mastered the UI, but the moment a client asks for real-time data or custom workflow automation, your project hits a wall. You are stuck in the 'basic app' trap, building shells that look good but lack the engine to drive real business value. It is the invisible ceiling of modern freelancing: the gap between a web designer and a true AI Builder.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to spend days wrestling with documentation or debugging syntax errors. With the right orchestration between Antigravity and Claude Code, you can bridge that gap instantly. This isn't just about adding a feature; it is about leveling up your architectural capabilities to ensure every build is professional, scalable, and ready to impress even your toughest clients.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, define the schema. A clear contract prevents integration nightmares.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust OpenAPI 3.0 specification for a SaaS integration that handles user authentication and data retrieval from a RESTful backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create an openapi.yaml file based on the provided schema definition'

steps.title: Bootstrap Service Layer
steps.description: Generate the boilerplate service code to handle authentication and error states automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, modular service layer in TypeScript that imports the openapi.yaml and provides a type-safe client for API communication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a service/api.ts file using the openapi.yaml definitions'

steps.title: Implement Middleware Logic
steps.description: Ensure your API calls are secure and performant by injecting middleware for logging and retries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement an axios-based middleware that adds exponential backoff for failed requests and logs errors to a standard monitoring format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor api.ts to include interceptors for retry logic and error logging'

steps.title: Map Data to UI Components
steps.description: Connect your API response to your frontend components for seamless data binding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook that manages the loading, error, and data states for the API endpoint defined in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create hooks/useApiData.ts and integrate into the main dashboard component'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suite
steps.description: Verify your integration with automated tests before handing over the project to your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Vitest unit tests that mock the API responses and verify the UI state transitions for success and failure cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate tests/api.test.ts and execute them to ensure 90% coverage'

pro_tips: Always use 'zod' schemas to validate API responses at the runtime edge, preventing type mismatch crashes.
Implement 'SWR' or 'React Query' for automatic caching; never fetch data manually on every mount.
Use 'Environment Variables' for all secrets, even in development, to build the habit of secure deployment.
Mock your API endpoints early using MSW (Mock Service Worker) so you can build the UI before the backend is finished.
Version your API requests in the header to ensure future updates don't break your client's production environment.
content.next_steps: Deploy your integrated app to a staging environment, run a load test, and present the live link to your client to demonstrate production-ready capability.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps that don't scale. Leverage these tools to build professional, high-impact software that commands premium rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
